0.question: What is Autolykos?
0.answer: Autolykos v2 is Ergo's memory-hard Proof-of-Work algorithm designed to be ASIC-resistant and GPU-friendly, keeping mining decentralized.

1.question: Why is Autolykos ASIC-resistant?
1.answer: Autolykos requires significant memory bandwidth making ASIC development economically unviable while remaining efficient on consumer GPUs.

2.question: How energy efficient is Autolykos?
2.answer: Autolykos is more energy-efficient than Bitcoin's SHA-256, requiring less electricity per hash while maintaining security.

3.question: Can I mine Ergo with my GPU?
3.answer: Yes! Autolykos is designed for GPU mining. Most modern GPUs can mine ERG profitably, making it accessible to home miners.

4.question: How does Autolykos prevent centralization?
4.answer: The memory-hard nature of Autolykos makes it expensive to develop specialized ASICs, keeping mining accessible to individual miners with consumer hardware.

5.question: What hardware do I need to mine Ergo?
5.answer: You need a modern GPU with at least 4GB VRAM. Popular choices include NVIDIA RTX series and AMD RX series graphics cards.

eutxoModel.codeExampleDescription: The eUTXO model simplifies smart contract development by treating everything as boxes with clear spending conditions. No complex state management, no re-entrancy concerns — just straightforward logic.

0.question: How is eUTXO different from Bitcoin's basic UTXO?
0.answer: While Bitcoin's UTXO model only tracks value transfers, Ergo's eUTXO extends this with arbitrary data storage in boxes and Turing-complete smart contracts via ErgoScript. This enables complex DeFi applications while keeping UTXO benefits.

1.question: Can eUTXO handle complex DeFi applications?
1.answer: Yes! Ergo has DEXes (Spectrum), lending protocols, stablecoins (SigmaUSD), and more. The eUTXO model actually makes these more secure by preventing re-entrancy and enabling parallel execution.

2.question: Why is the eUTXO model more scalable?
2.answer: Independent boxes enable parallel transaction processing. Unlike account models where state contention forces sequential execution, eUTXO transactions touching different boxes can run simultaneously.

3.question: How do smart contracts work in eUTXO?
3.answer: Each box has a guard script that defines its spending conditions. When spending a box, the script is executed with provided inputs. If it returns true, the box can be spent. This is simpler and more predictable than account-based execution.

0.question: What exactly is a velvet fork?
0.answer: A velvet fork is Ergo's approach to protocol upgrades that maintains complete backward compatibility while enabling new features. Unlike hard or soft forks, velvet forks never break existing functionality.

1.question: How do velvet forks differ from soft forks?
1.answer: While soft forks tighten existing rules, velvet forks can add entirely new features without affecting nodes that don't upgrade. This allows for more flexible protocol evolution.

2.question: Are velvet forks secure?
2.answer: Yes, velvet forks maintain the same security guarantees as the base protocol. New features are designed to be optional and don't compromise the security of existing functionality.

3.question: Do I need to upgrade my node for velvet forks?
3.answer: No, upgrades are optional. Your node will continue to work normally, though you won't have access to new features until you choose to upgrade.

whyFees.title: Why Are Transaction Fees Necessary on Ergo?
whyFees.description: Like most blockchains, transaction fees on Ergo play a crucial role. They are needed for:

miners.title: Rewarding Miners
miners.description: Miners expend computational resources to process transactions and secure the network. Fees are part of their compensation.

spam.title: Preventing Spam
spam.description: Fees make sending mass volumes of useless transactions economically unviable, protecting the network from congestion.

network.title: Network Operation
network.description: They contribute to maintaining a healthy and functional infrastructure.

standardPayment.title: Standard Payment in ERG
standardPayment.description: Typically, transaction fees on the Ergo network are paid in the platform's native coin – ERG. The fee amount depends on the transaction's "size" in bytes and current network congestion, but generally, Ergo fees aim to be predictable thanks to the eUTXO model.

introduction.title: Introducing Babel Fees (EIP-0019): Flexible Fee Payments

whatAre.title: What are Babel Fees?
whatAre.description: Babel Fees are an innovative mechanism on Ergo that allows users to pay transaction fees using native tokens issued on the Ergo blockchain instead of ERG. Imagine being able to pay for postage not just with cash (ERG), but also with valuable collectible stamps (your tokens).

whyCreated.title: Why were Babel Fees created? (The Problem Solved)

ux.title: Improved User Experience (UX)
ux.description: Users holding specific tokens (e.g., tokens of a particular dApp) might not always have enough ERG readily available to pay fees. This creates friction and barriers.

utility.title: Increased Token Utility
utility.description: Babel Fees significantly enhance the utility of native Ergo tokens, as they can be used for a fundamental network operation – paying fees.

accessibility.title: dApp Accessibility
accessibility.description: Simplifies interaction with decentralized applications that heavily utilize their own native tokens.

howItWorks.title: How Babel Fees Work (Simplified Explanation)
howItWorks.description: The Babel Fees mechanism is quite elegant and doesn't require changes to the core protocol regarding how fees are ultimately received (the network still receives fees in ERG).

userOffer.title: User's Offer
userOffer.description: A user wishing to pay a fee with tokens creates a special "output" in their transaction, often referred to as a "Babel fee box." In this box, they offer a certain amount of their tokens in exchange for someone else covering their ERG fee.

detecting.title: 1. Detecting the Offer
detecting.description: Miners or specialized bots/services (intermediaries) scan the network for these "Babel fee boxes."

executing.title: 2. Executing the Exchange
executing.description: If the token offer is profitable (i.e., the market value of the offered tokens is greater than or equal to the ERG fee amount plus a small profit for the intermediary), the intermediary "fulfills" this offer.

paying.title: 3. Paying the ERG Fee
paying.description: The intermediary takes the offered tokens from the "Babel fee box" and pays the required transaction fee in ERG to the miners who include the transaction in a block.

market.title: Market Mechanism
market.description: The effectiveness of Babel Fees for a specific token depends on a "market" existing for it – meaning there must be intermediaries willing to accept these tokens in exchange for ERG at a certain rate. The more popular a token, the higher the likelihood it will be accepted for fee payment.

convenience.title: Convenience
convenience.description: Ability to pay fees with tokens you already own, without needing to first exchange them for ERG.

friction.title: Reduced Friction
friction.description: Simplifies the use of dApps that are centered around their own tokens.

utility.title: Increased Token Utility
utility.description: Makes their tokens more in-demand and integrated into the ecosystem.

ux.title: Improved UX
ux.description: Better experience for their application users.

benefits.userFriendly: A more user-friendly and versatile platform.
benefits.tokenEconomy: Stimulation of an active token economy.

walletSupport.title: Wallet Support
walletSupport.description: Find out which Ergo wallets (e.g., Nautilus Wallet or others) and dApps support the creation of transactions using Babel Fees. This feature may not be available in all wallets.

tokenAcceptance.title: Token Acceptance
tokenAcceptance.description: Not all tokens may be automatically accepted. This depends on intermediaries being willing to exchange them for ERG.

exchangeRates.title: Exchange Rates
exchangeRates.description: The "price" or exchange rate at which your tokens are accepted to cover ERG fees will be determined by market conditions and intermediary offers.

howToUse.title: How to Use
howToUse.description: When constructing a transaction in a compatible wallet or dApp, you might see an option to select a token to pay the fee. The interface will typically show the required amount of tokens based on current market rates.

howMaintainPeg.question: How do algorithmic stablecoins maintain their peg?
howMaintainPeg.answer: Algorithmic stablecoins on Ergo maintain their peg through a combination of over-collateralization, algorithmic supply adjustments, and market incentives. The AgeUSD protocol uses a dual-token system where reserve providers absorb volatility to maintain stability.

whyErgoIdeal.question: What makes Ergo ideal for stablecoins?
whyErgoIdeal.answer: Ergo's eUTXO model provides superior security for collateral management, native oracle pools ensure reliable price feeds, and ErgoScript enables complex stability mechanisms while maintaining transparency and auditability.

howAgeUsdWorks.question: How does the AgeUSD protocol work?
howAgeUsdWorks.answer: AgeUSD uses a reserve pool of ERG collateral and two tokens: SigmaUSD (stable) and SigmaRSV (reserve). Reserve providers mint SigmaRSV to add collateral and earn from fees and price appreciation, while users can mint SigmaUSD when the reserve ratio is healthy.

risks.question: What are the risks of algorithmic stablecoins?
risks.answer: Main risks include under-collateralization during extreme market conditions, oracle manipulation, smart contract bugs, and liquidity crises. Ergo mitigates these through robust reserve requirements, decentralized oracles, and formal verification capabilities.

buildOwn.question: Can I build my own stablecoin on Ergo?
buildOwn.answer: Yes! Ergo provides all necessary infrastructure: oracle pools for price feeds, ErgoScript for stability mechanisms, and proven protocols like AgeUSD as reference implementations. The community offers extensive documentation and support.

howErgoMixerWorks.question: How does ErgoMixer provide privacy?
howErgoMixerWorks.answer: ErgoMixer uses a CoinJoin++ protocol that allows users to mix their coins with others without trusting a central party. It leverages Ergo's Sigma protocols to create complex spending conditions that ensure privacy while maintaining security.

whatAreSigmaProtocols.question: What are Sigma protocols?
whatAreSigmaProtocols.answer: Sigma protocols are a class of zero-knowledge proofs that allow proving knowledge of a secret without revealing it. Ergo's ErgoScript natively supports Sigma protocols, enabling complex privacy-preserving applications without trusted setups.

howStealthAddressesWork.question: How do stealth addresses work on Ergo?
howStealthAddressesWork.answer: Stealth addresses allow receivers to publish a single address while senders generate unique one-time addresses for each payment. This breaks the link between the published address and on-chain transactions, enhancing receiver privacy.

isPrivacyOptional.question: Is Ergo privacy optional or mandatory?
isPrivacyOptional.answer: Ergo provides optional privacy features. Users can choose when to use privacy tools like ErgoMixer or stealth addresses based on their needs, maintaining flexibility while ensuring regulatory compliance when needed.

whatMakesErgoDifferent.question: What makes Ergo's privacy different from other chains?
whatMakesErgoDifferent.answer: Ergo's eUTXO model naturally provides better privacy than account-based systems. Combined with native Sigma protocols, optional privacy tools, and no trusted setups, Ergo offers a unique balance of privacy, transparency, and compliance.

whatIsRosenBridge.answer: Rosen Bridge is Ergo's flagship decentralized bridge solution. It uses a network of watchers and guards to facilitate trustless asset transfers between Ergo and other blockchains without central points of failure.

areBridgedAssetsSecure.question: Are bridged assets secure?
areBridgedAssetsSecure.answer: Yes, bridged assets are secured through multi-signature contracts and decentralized validator networks. The original assets are locked in secure contracts, and wrapped versions maintain 1:1 backing.

howEfficientMinting.question: How efficient is NFT minting on Ergo?
howEfficientMinting.answer: Ergo supports batch minting, allowing creators to mint entire collections in a single transaction. The eUTXO model makes this extremely efficient compared to account-based chains.

howOperatorsIncentivized.answer: Oracle operators earn rewards for providing accurate and timely data. They must stake collateral that can be slashed for malicious behavior, creating economic incentives for honest operation.

howReliablePriceFeeds.question: How reliable are oracle price feeds?
howReliablePriceFeeds.answer: Ergo's oracle pools use multiple independent data sources and consensus mechanisms to ensure reliability. Historical data shows 99.9%+ uptime with accurate price feeds updated every epoch.

0.question: How is eUTXO on Ergo different from the account model?
0.answer: eUTXO splits the blockchain state into independent boxes (outputs), enabling parallel smart contracts without a global state and reducing reentrancy risks. DApps become composable: complex logic can be built from simple outputs.

1.question: What is ErgoScript and why is it secure?
1.answer: ErgoScript is Ergo's contract language for programmable money with formally verifiable code, strict typing, and built-in cryptographic primitives. Contracts are auditable and predictable—design avoids global mutable state and typical reentrancy patterns.

2.question: Why is Autolykos considered decentralized and energy-efficient?
2.answer: Autolykos is memory-hard and ASIC-resistant, making it fair for ordinary miners. It rewards solo mining and reduces pool dependence, keeping the network secure with moderate energy use.

3.question: What is Storage Rent and why does it matter?
3.answer: Storage Rent is a state recycling mechanism where unspent coins after ~4 years start paying rent. This prevents unlimited blockchain growth and ensures miners earn revenue even after emissions end.

4.question: What privacy and multisig features are built in?
4.answer: Sigma protocols provide native zero-knowledge proofs, ring signatures, multisig/threshold schemes, and private payments without external hacks. This simplifies building confidential dApps.

5.question: What do NIPoPoWs enable?
5.answer: NIPoPoWs create tiny blockchain proofs that allow light clients, instant mobile sync, and trustless cross-chain bridges without full nodes.

6.question: How are Oracle Pools different from traditional oracles?
6.answer: Oracle Pools are protocol-native, composable data feeds without a central operator—no single point of bribery or failure. They can be plugged into any contract.

7.question: What are Subblocks and why use them?
7.answer: Subblocks split blocks into fast sub-blocks to speed up payment and dApp confirmation without trusted sequencers. This provides Layer-1 scaling with faster perceived confirmations.

8.question: Can I issue tokens and NFTs without a smart contract?
8.answer: Yes—issuance is native at the protocol layer, in a single transaction. True native assets are instantly composable with dApps.

whyStorageRent.question: Why does Ergo charge "storage rent"? Do I have to pay just to hold ERG?
whyStorageRent.answer: No, this isn't a holding tax—it's a way to keep the blockchain "clean." Fees apply only to coins (UTXOs) left untouched for more than 4 years. Think of it like long-term storage: if you abandon a box and never return, a tiny fee is charged for the space you occupy. This keeps the network from getting clogged with forgotten data.

longTermInvestor.question: Can I lose my funds if I'm a long-term investor (HODLer)?
longTermInvestor.answer: Almost impossible. Any action—like sending your coins, even to yourself—resets the 4-year timer. The rent is so small it only affects very low-value UTXOs ("dust"). The system is designed to clean up lost change, not to penalize active users or investors.

whyImportant.question: Why is it so important to get rid of "blockchain dust"?
whyImportant.answer: It directly affects decentralization. Every forgotten transaction increases the size of the blockchain state, which full nodes must store and process. Without cleanup, running a full node would become too expensive over time, leaving control to big corporations. Storage rent is a long-term guarantee that Ergo stays accessible and truly decentralized.

whereRentGoes.question: Where does the collected rent go? Does anyone profit from it?
whereRentGoes.answer: The fees aren't kept by a company or burned—they're paid out to miners. This gives miners a direct incentive to help clean up the blockchain state, which aligns perfectly with the community's long-term interests. It's a self-sustaining economic model that keeps the blockchain healthy and resilient.

technicalSection.title: Technical Details
technicalSection.description: Understanding the architecture and implementation of subblocks

technicalDetails.title: Architecture
technicalDetails.content: Subblocks are intermediate blocks between main blocks that provide faster confirmations. They maintain the same security model as main blocks but with adjusted difficulty targets.

technicalDetails.title: Consensus Mechanism
technicalDetails.content: Uses a modified version of Autolykos that allows for variable difficulty targets. Subblocks contribute to the overall chain weight while maintaining compatibility with existing infrastructure.

technicalDetails.title: Transaction Ordering
technicalDetails.content: Transactions in subblocks are ordered deterministically using a combination of timestamp and transaction ID, preventing front-running and ensuring fairness.

technicalDetails.title: State Management
technicalDetails.content: Subblocks update the UTXO set incrementally, allowing for efficient state transitions without full block validation overhead.

faqSection.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqSection.description: Common questions about subblocks and their implementation

faq.question: What are subblocks?
faq.answer: Subblocks are intermediate blocks between main blocks that provide faster confirmations while maintaining the same security model. They enable sub-second transaction finality without sacrificing decentralization.

faq.question: How fast are subblock confirmations?
faq.answer: Subblocks provide confirmations in approximately 1 second, compared to the 2-minute main block time, enabling near-instant transaction finality for improved user experience.

faq.question: Do subblocks require a hard fork?
faq.answer: No, subblocks are designed to be backward compatible. Existing infrastructure continues to work while new features are gradually adopted.

faq.question: How do subblocks improve scalability?
faq.answer: Subblocks enable 10x throughput increase through parallel processing and more frequent block production while maintaining Layer 1 security guarantees.

faq.question: Are subblocks secure?
faq.answer: Yes, subblocks maintain the same security model as main blocks with adjusted difficulty targets. They contribute to overall chain weight and are protected by the same PoW consensus.

whatIsAutolykos.question: What is Autolykos v2?
whatIsAutolykos.answer: Autolykos v2 is Ergo's memory-hard, GPU-friendly Proof-of-Work algorithm designed to keep mining decentralized and accessible.

asicProof.question: Is Ergo's PoW completely ASIC-proof?
asicProof.answer: No. It's ASIC-resistant (not ASIC-proof). By making memory bandwidth/latency the bottleneck, specialized-hardware advantages are limited and GPUs remain competitive.

minerSoftware.question: Which miner software works with Autolykos v2?
minerSoftware.answer: See the Mining Guide for current tooling and setup steps. It covers supported miners, configuration basics, and troubleshooting.

poolVsSolo.question: Pool or solo mining?
poolVsSolo.answer: Both are possible. Pools smooth rewards; solo depends on luck and has higher variance. Choose based on your risk tolerance and expected uptime.

hashratePower.question: Typical hashrate/watt ranges?
hashratePower.answer: They vary by GPU model, drivers, and settings. Start with stock settings, ensure stability and thermals, and consult community resources for optimization.

environmentalImpact.question: Is this approach greener?
environmentalImpact.answer: Memory-hardness dampens the hardware arms race and extends useful GPU lifecycles, which helps reduce e-waste and unnecessary upgrades.

items.question: How does ErgoScript differ from Solidity?
items.answer: ErgoScript is functional, based on eUTXO model, and has built-in formal verification. Solidity is imperative, account-based, and requires external tools for verification.

items.question: Is ErgoScript Turing complete?
items.answer: ErgoScript is Turing complete within reasonable bounds. It prevents infinite loops through cost models and execution limits.

items.question: Can I use existing libraries?
items.answer: Yes, ErgoScript has a growing ecosystem of libraries and templates for common patterns like multisig, timelocks, and oracles.

items.question: How do I get started with ErgoScript?
items.answer: Start with the official documentation, try the online playground, and join the developer community on Discord for support.

whatFeatures.question: What privacy features does Ergo offer?
whatFeatures.answer: Ergo offers multiple privacy features including Sigma protocols, ring signatures, zero-knowledge proofs, and confidential transactions. These technologies enable privacy-preserving transactions while maintaining security and compliance.

howSigmaWorks.question: How do Sigma protocols work?
howSigmaWorks.answer: Sigma protocols are zero-knowledge proof systems that allow proving statements about secret values without revealing the values themselves. They enable privacy-preserving authentication and transaction validation.

regulatoryCompliant.question: Are Ergo's privacy features regulatory compliant?
regulatoryCompliant.answer: Yes, Ergo's privacy features are designed to be regulatory compliant. They provide selective disclosure capabilities, allowing users to reveal transaction details when required while maintaining privacy by default.

whyPrivacyMatters.title: Why Privacy Matters
whyPrivacyMatters.description1: In an increasingly digital world, financial privacy is not about hiding wrongdoing — it's about protecting your fundamental rights. Privacy enables freedom of expression, protects against discrimination, and ensures that your financial activities remain your own business.
whyPrivacyMatters.description2: Ergo recognizes that privacy and transparency can coexist. You should have the choice to keep your transactions private while still participating in a transparent, auditable blockchain ecosystem.

privacyLevel.question: What level of privacy does Ergo offer? Is it just coin mixing?
privacyLevel.answer: Ergo offers multi-layered privacy, not just a single feature. At its core are Sigma protocols—powerful cryptography that allows data to be hidden. On top of this runs ErgoMixer (non-custodial community tool), the first non-interactive and trustless mixer in the eUTXO space. It helps break the link between your transactions, providing strong anonymity on-chain.

suspicious.question: If I use privacy features, won't my transactions look suspicious?
suspicious.answer: On the contrary. Because privacy tools are a core part of the Ergo ecosystem, using them is the norm. When many people mix their coins, they create a large anonymity set where it's easy to blend in. On Ergo, protecting your financial data is standard practice, not a reason for suspicion.

criminals.question: Doesn't this kind of privacy attract criminals?
criminals.answer: Privacy is a fundamental right, not an admission of guilt. You don't use curtains on your windows because you're doing something illegal. Businesses need privacy for trade secrets and payrolls, and individuals need it to protect themselves from tracking and profiling.

auditProof.question: What if I need to prove a transaction for taxes or an audit?
auditProof.answer: Ergo applies a 'privacy by choice' approach with selective disclosure. When needed, you can provide viewing keys for specific transactions to an auditor or regulator without revealing your entire financial history.

0.question: How does eUTXO differ from Bitcoin's UTXO model?
0.answer: eUTXO extends Bitcoin's UTXO with registers (R0-R9) for data storage and ErgoTree guard scripts for programmable spending conditions. This enables smart contracts while maintaining UTXO's security and parallelism benefits.

1.question: What makes Sigma protocols special for blockchain?
1.answer: Sigma protocols provide zero-knowledge proofs without trusted setup, enabling privacy features like ring signatures and confidential transactions. They're built directly into ErgoScript, making advanced cryptography accessible to developers.

2.question: Why does Ergo need Storage Rent?
2.answer: Storage Rent prevents blockchain bloat by recycling fees from unused UTXOs back to miners. This ensures long-term sustainability and predictable storage costs, solving the 'state explosion' problem other blockchains face.

3.question: How do light clients trust the network without full validation?
3.answer: NIPoPoWs (Non-Interactive Proofs of Proof-of-Work) provide cryptographically secure proofs that a transaction is included in the blockchain. Light clients can verify these logarithmic-size proofs without downloading the entire chain.

4.question: What are the main dApp development patterns on Ergo?
4.answer: Key patterns include: Order books for DEXs using box chaining, Oracle pools for data feeds with consensus mechanisms, DAO governance with voting and quorum logic, and Multi-stage contracts for complex workflows.

5.question: How does Autolykos ensure decentralization?
5.answer: Autolykos is memory-hard and ASIC-resistant, favoring consumer GPUs over specialized hardware. This keeps mining accessible to individuals and prevents centralization in ASIC farms.

6.question: What are Ergo's scalability characteristics?
6.answer: eUTXO enables parallel transaction processing, NIPoPoWs provide efficient light clients, and Storage Rent prevents state bloat. Layer 2 solutions can leverage these properties for further scaling.

7.question: How do native tokens work without smart contracts?
7.answer: Native tokens are built into the protocol layer, stored directly in UTXO boxes. This provides efficient creation, transfer, and management without requiring smart contract deployment or execution.

intro.title: ERGO PATH FINDER
intro.subtitle: DISCOVER YOUR PLACE IN THE ECOSYSTEM
intro.description: Welcome to Ergo! New to our vast ecosystem and not sure where to begin? This short quiz will help you identify which aspects of Ergo might interest you most and guide you to personalized resources.
intro.path_description: Answer a few simple questions to find your path: Are you a future Investor, Developer, Miner, NFT Enthusiast, DeFi User, or something else?

q1.question: What primarily attracts you to blockchain technology and Ergo?

options.building: Building applications and contributing to new technologies
options.mining: Supporting network security and earning rewards through hardware
options.nft: Collecting, creating, or trading unique digital items and art
options.defi: Using new financial tools for borrowing, lending, or trading
options.privacy: Enhancing my financial privacy and anonymity
options.learning: I'm new and just want to learn the basics first!

q2.question: How would you describe your technical comfort level?

options.very_comfortable: Very comfortable; I can code or manage complex systems
options.moderately_comfortable: Moderately comfortable; I can learn new software and follow guides
options.not_technical: Not very technical; I prefer simple and user-friendly applications

q3.question: What kind of activity within Ergo are you most interested in?

options.growing_assets: Growing my assets and exploring financial instruments
options.creating_tools: Creating new tools, dApps, or smart contracts
options.securing_network: Securing the network and earning ERG
options.understanding_tech: Deeply understanding the technology and its principles

q4.question: If you're interested in finance/investment, what's your approach to new financial technologies?

options.established_low_risk: Looking for established, lower-risk ways to engage
options.defi_opportunities: Exploring DeFi opportunities while understanding the risks
options.cutting_edge: Interested in cutting-edge, experimental financial tools
options.not_interested: Not particularly interested in financial aspects

q5.question: If you want to contribute, what type of participation inspires you most?

options.creating_content: Creating educational content, art, or marketing materials
options.helping_community: Helping others in the community and answering questions
options.governance: Participating in governance discussions and proposals

comparison.title: Platform Comparison
comparison.subtitle: How Ergo compares to other blockchains

keyDifferentiators.title: Predictable & Auditable Security
keyDifferentiators.ergo: No known protocol-level reentrancy class; predictable execution costs
keyDifferentiators.others: Reentrancy patterns and gas price auctions common in account models

keyDifferentiators.title: Native Privacy
keyDifferentiators.ergo: Sigma protocols for threshold/multisig and privacy; ring-like constructions possible
keyDifferentiators.others: Often requires external add-ons or trusted services

keyDifferentiators.title: Developer Experience
keyDifferentiators.others: Mutable global state; complex reentrancy-safe patterns

hero.subtitle: A next‑generation, ASIC‑resistant Proof‑of‑Work platform with predictable, auditable smart contracts and native privacy.

tech_article.headline: Ergo — PoW smart‑contract platform (eUTXO & Sigma Protocols)
tech_article.description: Introduction to Ergo: ASIC-resistant PoW, eUTXO smart contracts, Sigma (ZK) protocols, storage rent, NIPoPoWs.

what_is_ergo.title: What is
what_is_ergo.description_1: Ergo is designed for developing secure and powerful decentralized applications (dApps) and financial contracts. It builds on a decade of blockchain theory and development, combining established principles with new research.
what_is_ergo.description_2: Its mission is to provide tools for decentralized and accessible financial systems — empowering people with economic freedom without intermediaries. Learn about the

items.eutxo_contracts: eUTXO smart contracts with predictable execution
items.sigma_protocols: Sigma (ZK) protocols for threshold/multisig and privacy

key_innovation.title: Key Innovation
key_innovation.description: Ergo combines the security of Bitcoin's UTXO model with the expressiveness of Ethereum's smart contracts, creating a unique platform for complex financial applications.

privacy_first.title: Privacy First
privacy_first.description: Built-in privacy at the protocol level

storage_rent.title: Storage Rent
storage_rent.description: Sustainable economic model for long-term viability

decentralized_first.title: Decentralized First
decentralized_first.description: Launched fairly with no premine or ICO. Community-driven for maximum decentralization.

secure_reliable.title: Secure & Reliable
secure_reliable.description: Built on Proof-of-Work consensus (Autolykos) and the innovative eUTXO model.

powerful_contracts.title: Powerful Contracts
powerful_contracts.description: ErgoScript enables secure financial apps that are hard to implement safely in account-based models.

long_term_focus.title: Long-term Focus
long_term_focus.description: Designed for resilience with focus on real-world utility over speculation.

core_principles.title: Core
core_principles.subtitle: Built on fundamental values that ensure long-term sustainability and real-world utility

proof_of_work.title: Proof-of-Work (PoW)
proof_of_work.subtitle: Maximum security
proof_of_work.description: Autolykos algorithm is ASIC-resistant, ensuring fair distribution and robust security.

extended_utxo.title: Extended UTXO
extended_utxo.subtitle: Smart money
extended_utxo.description: Allows complex smart contracts with better scalability and predictable fees.

sigma_protocols.title: Sigma Protocols

eutxo_model.question: What is the eUTXO model?

sigma_protocols.question: What are Sigma protocols on Ergo?

proof_of_work.question: Why Proof‑of‑Work for Ergo?

guides.sigma_protocols_are_advanced_cryptograph: Sigma protocols are advanced cryptographic tools that enable zero-knowledge proofs, allowing users to prove statements without revealing information. This is the foundation for privacy in Ergo smart contracts.
guides.ergomixer_is_a_privacy_tool: ErgoMixer is a privacy tool that allows users to mix their ERG and tokens, breaking the on-chain link between sender and receiver. This enhances transaction privacy and fungibility.

consensus.autolykos_is_a_memory_hard: Autolykos is a memory-hard PoW algorithm designed to be resistant to ASICs and favor GPU miners. It helps maintain decentralization and prevents mining centralization.

eutxo_reentrancy.question: How does eUTXO reduce reentrancy risk?
eutxo_reentrancy.answer: Transactions consume specified UTXOs and cannot be re-entered; the shared-state pattern behind reentrancy is absent.

predictable_fees.question: Are fees predictable on Ergo?
predictable_fees.answer: Script cost is bounded and fees are predictable; there are no gas price auctions (final fees depend on mempool conditions).

light_clients.question: Does Ergo have light clients?
light_clients.answer: Yes. NIPoPoWs enable succinct proofs for trust-minimized light clients.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about Ergo blockchain, technology, and ecosystem.
faq.noResults: No questions found matching your search.

whatIsErgo.a: Ergo is a next-generation smart contract platform that prioritizes economic freedom through decentralized, censorship-resistant, and secure financial contracts.

howDifferent.q: How is Ergo different from other blockchains?
howDifferent.a: Ergo combines proven cryptographic features with innovative approaches like eUTXO model, storage rent, and Sigma protocols for enhanced security and sustainability.

ergToken.a: ERG is Ergo's native cryptocurrency used for transaction fees, smart contract execution, and storage rent. It has a fixed supply cap of 97.7 million tokens.

eUTXO.a: Extended UTXO (eUTXO) combines Bitcoin's UTXO security with Ethereum's smart contract expressiveness, enabling more predictable and secure dApps.

autolykos.a: Autolykos v2 is Ergo's ASIC-resistant Proof-of-Work algorithm designed to be memory-hard and promote decentralized mining.

sigmaProtocols.a: Sigma protocols enable powerful cryptographic proofs for privacy and complex smart contracts without revealing sensitive information.

getErg.q: How do I get ERG tokens?
getErg.a: You can buy ERG on exchanges like KuCoin, Gate.io, or CoinEx, or earn them through mining or participating in the ecosystem.

wallet.a: Popular options include Nautilus (browser extension), official mobile wallets, or hardware wallets like Ledger for maximum security.

startMining.q: How do I start mining ERG?
startMining.a: You can mine ERG using GPU miners. Check our mining guide for pool recommendations and setup instructions.

dApps.q: What dApps are available on Ergo?
dApps.a: Ergo has DeFi protocols like SigmaUSD, DEXs like Spectrum, NFT platforms, and various tools. Explore the ecosystem page for more.

defi.a: Ergo offers algorithmic stablecoins (SigmaUSD), decentralized exchanges, lending protocols, and yield farming opportunities.

nfts.a: Yes! Ergo has a vibrant NFT ecosystem with marketplaces, creation tools, and unique features like royalty enforcement.

getHelp.a: Join our Discord, Telegram, or visit the Ergo Forum. The community is welcoming and always ready to help newcomers.

contribute.q: How can I contribute to Ergo?
contribute.a: You can contribute through development, documentation, community support, content creation, or applying for ecosystem grants.

stayUpdated.q: How do I stay updated with Ergo news?
stayUpdated.a: Follow @ergoplatformorg on Twitter, join our Discord/Telegram, subscribe to the newsletter, or check the official blog.

content.paragraph1: In a world of increasing surveillance and control, we stand for financial freedom and privacy.
content.paragraph2: Ergo is built on the core principles that drove the original crypto revolution: open, permissionless, secure systems that empower regular people.

whereToBy.question: Where can I buy ERG?
whereToBy.answer: You can buy ERG on centralized exchanges like KuCoin and Gate.io, decentralized exchanges like Spectrum Finance, or P2P platforms.

minAmount.question: What's the minimum amount to buy?
minAmount.answer: Minimum amounts vary by platform. Most exchanges allow purchases starting from $10-20 worth of ERG.

fees.question: What are typical fees?
fees.answer: Exchange fees range from 0.1% to 0.5% per trade.

storage.question: How do I store ERG safely?
storage.answer: Use official Ergo wallets like Ergo Wallet, Nautilus, or hardware wallets for maximum security.

whereStore.question: Where should I store my ERG?
whereStore.answer: Use official Ergo wallets like Nautilus, SAFEW, or Ergo Mobile for secure storage.

minPurchase.question: What's the minimum ERG purchase?
minPurchase.answer: Minimum varies by exchange, typically 1-10 ERG.

time.question: How long does it take?
time.answer: CEX purchases are instant, DEX swaps take 2-5 minutes.

babelFees.title: Babel Fees - Pay Transaction Fees with Any Token
babelFees.description: Learn about Babel Fees on Ergo blockchain - pay transaction fees using any token instead of ERG
babelFees.subDescription: Babel Fees allow users to pay transaction fees with tokens other than ERG, improving user experience and token utility

seo.title: Babel Fees - Flexible Transaction Fee Payment | Ergo Platform
seo.description: Discover how Babel Fees on Ergo blockchain enable paying transaction fees with any token. Learn about the benefits, implementation, and use cases.
seo.keywords: Babel Fees, Ergo blockchain, transaction fees, token payment, ERG alternative, flexible fees, DeFi Ergo

cta.experienceFlexibility: Experience the flexibility of paying fees with any token

whyFees.title: Why Transaction Fees Are Necessary
whyFees.description: Transaction fees are essential for maintaining network security and preventing spam attacks on the blockchain.
whyFees.spamDescription: Fees prevent network congestion from spam
whyFees.standardPaymentDescription: Typically, fees must be paid in ERG (native token)

whatAreBabelFees.title: What Are Babel Fees
whatAreBabelFees.whatAreDescription: Babel Fees are a mechanism that allows users to pay transaction fees with any token instead of ERG.
whatAreBabelFees.uxImprovementDescription: Users don't need to hold ERG for transactions

howBabelFeesWork.title: How Babel Fees Work
howBabelFeesWork.description: Babel Fees work through a system of intermediaries who accept tokens and pay fees in ERG.
howBabelFeesWork.userOfferDescription: User specifies token and amount to pay fee
howBabelFeesWork.intermediaryPayDescription: Intermediary pays ERG to miners, receives tokens
howBabelFeesWork.marketMechanismDescription: Competition between intermediaries ensures fair exchange rates

userExperience.title: Better User Experience
userExperience.description: No need to hold ERG for transactions

tokenUtility.title: Increased Token Utility
tokenUtility.description: Use your tokens directly for fees

accessibility.title: Greater Accessibility
accessibility.description: Lower barrier to entry for new users

practicalConsiderations.title: Practical Considerations
practicalConsiderations.tokenAcceptanceDescription: Not all tokens may be accepted

currentStatus.title: Current Status
currentStatus.importantDescription: Babel Fees are in development and implementation phase
currentStatus.widelyAdopted: Will Babel Fees be widely adopted by the ecosystem?
currentStatus.activeMarkets: Will active markets emerge for token-to-fee exchanges?
currentStatus.ongoingWork: Ongoing work on standardization and implementation

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.description: Babel Fees represent an innovative approach to transaction fee payment, making Ergo blockchain more accessible and user-friendly.
conclusion.keepEye: Keep an eye on this technology's development in the Ergo ecosystem!

howItWorks.description: Babel Fees enable flexible fee payments through smart contracts

howWork.question: How do Babel Fees work?
howWork.answer: Smart contracts automatically convert your chosen token to ERG for fee payment.

whichTokens.question: Which tokens are supported?
whichTokens.answer: Any token can potentially support Babel Fees if liquidity providers enable it.
